meaning: adj.泛希腊的,全大学校友会的
examples: A variety of informal and formal games were popular in Ancient Greece, with the most prestigious ones achieving Panhellenic status.
The once powerful Panhellenic Socialist Movement has plunged to 8%, a record low, say the polls.
Fine painted vessels depict Panhellenic sporting events, mythical lovers, stage plays, a wedding and the joyful afterlife.
The Panhellenic Socialist Movement (Pasok), which also supports the bail-out, would come a distant third, with 11.8%.
